  • dyana.decode
  • dyana.decode.params

dyana.decode.params#

Decoder tuning parameters for constrained transition scoring.

Classes

DecodeTuningParams([speaker_switch_penalty, ...])

Small bundle of decode tuning knobs.
class dyana.decode.params.DecodeTuningParams(speaker_switch_penalty=-6.0, leak_entry_bias=-2.0, ovl_transition_cost=-3.0, a_to_ovl_cost=None, b_to_ovl_cost=None, ovl_to_a_cost=None, ovl_to_b_cost=None, ipu_detection_mode='balanced', silence_bias=0.0, merge_silence_gap_ms=400.0)[source]#

Bases: object

Small bundle of decode tuning knobs.

Notes

ovl_transition_cost is kept for backward compatibility and acts as a fallback when explicit OVL edge costs are not provided.

ipu_detection_mode="balanced" preserves the current decoder behavior. ipu_detection_mode="high_recall" biases the system toward speech coverage for pre-transcription IPU segmentation, even if that slightly over-extends IPUs across silence-like dips.

Parameters:

  • speaker_switch_penalty (float)

  • leak_entry_bias (float)

  • ovl_transition_cost (float)

  • a_to_ovl_cost (float | None)

  • b_to_ovl_cost (float | None)

  • ovl_to_a_cost (float | None)

  • ovl_to_b_cost (float | None)

  • ipu_detection_mode (Literal['balanced', 'high_recall'])

  • silence_bias (float)

  • merge_silence_gap_ms (float)

resolved_ovl_costs()[source]#

Return explicit OVL transition costs in A->OVL, B->OVL, OVL->A, OVL->B order.

Return type:

Tuple[float, float, float, float]

a_to_ovl_cost: float | None = None#
b_to_ovl_cost: float | None = None#
ipu_detection_mode: Literal['balanced', 'high_recall'] = 'balanced'#
leak_entry_bias: float = -2.0#
merge_silence_gap_ms: float = 400.0#
ovl_to_a_cost: float | None = None#
ovl_to_b_cost: float | None = None#
ovl_transition_cost: float = -3.0#
silence_bias: float = 0.0#
speaker_switch_penalty: float = -6.0#
